title: alcea striata subsp. rufescens
intro: Alcea striata subsp. rufescens, also known as the Red Striped Hollyhock, is a plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Alcea striata subsp. rufescens can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves for topical application, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
